summary: Suspicious plugin patterns found: plugin-manifest-risk. Current evidence is mainly config signals. Review before trust or enablement.

packageRecommendation.reason: This plugin has findings that need human review before trust (plugin manifest weakens trust defaults).

kindSensitiveNotes: Plugins have a larger runtime-trust surface. Packaged code, manifests, hooks, and persistence behavior matter more than README-only language.
This plugin manifest weakens trust or audit defaults. Review the governance posture it ships with, not just the code paths it exposes.
The strongest current signal is review-before-trust, not immediate blocking.

findings.title: Plugin manifest ships risky trust defaults
findings.summary: This OpenClaw plugin manifest enables permissive defaults on high-risk surfaces or weakens audit posture, which raises the trust bar before install.
findings.recommendation: Review the manifest defaults before trusting the plugin. Prefer require-consent, deny, or audit-forward defaults on risky surfaces.

groupedFindings.title: Plugin manifest weakens trust defaults
groupedFindings.summary: openclaw.plugin.json sets permissive defaults on risky plugin surfaces or weakens audit posture.
groupedFindings.exploitabilityReason: The plugin manifest weakens trust defaults, but the risk comes from permissive governance posture rather than automatic payload execution.
groupedFindings.recommendedActionReason: This plugin ships permissive trust defaults or weak audit defaults, so it should be reviewed before trust even without an auto-exec chain.
